mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 9.0%, it signals severe labor market deterioration that immediately triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0%, or the signal loses predictive power during high-volatility regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when RSAFS and HOUST move in opposite directions, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio remains below 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output that supports corporate earnings growth, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns during expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 20-day SPX returns during late-cycle phases, or the mechanism shows negative predictive accuracy when GDP growth exceeds 3.0% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls signal improving economic activity that consistently generates positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent five trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns across multiple market cycles, or the signal produces negative alpha after transaction costs.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation accelerates beyond 3.5% annually, it forces expectations of tighter monetary policy that compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in over 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL growth surpasses 3.5%, or the inflation threshold fails to predict return direction in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining industrial production and stagnant gross domestic product indicates supply-side bottlenecks that suppress corporate margins and drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns when both series trend upward, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real personal consumption expenditures reflect robust household spending that immediately boosts equity prices, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following week during recovery periods.
failure_condition: RSAFS acceleration fails to precede positive 5-day SPX returns when consumer confidence indices remain below 80, or the signal shows no predictive edge in out-of-sample backtests.
